channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government introduces strategies to support Tanzania’s cocoa sector amid global price slump
item.description: The Tanzanian government has introduced several strategies to stabilize and revive the cocoa sector after prices fell sharply, worrying farmers and industry stakeholders. Prices have dropped from arou...

item.title: Zanzibar Government Commits Sh800 Billion to Upgrade Electricity Infrastructure by 2028
item.description: Zanzibar is taking serious steps to fix the frequent electricity outages that have been troubling residents. The government has committed Sh800 billion to upgrade the islands' power infrastructure. Cu...

item.title: Milford FC reach Nedbank Cup semi-finals after dramatic penalty shoot-out against Sekhukhune United
item.description: Milford FC pulled off a remarkable upset in the Nedbank Cup quarter-finals, defeating Sekhukhune United after a tense penalty shoot-out at the Peter Mokaba Stadium in Polokwane. The KwaZulu-Natal side...

item.title: Trump sets terms for Iran amid escalating US-Israeli military strikes
item.description: US President Donald Trump has posted on social media that the US will only accept 'unconditional surrender' from Iran as part of ending military hostilities. The announcement came shortly after Irania...

item.title: Ex-IGP Egbetokun Makes Last-Minute Redeployments of Senior Police Officers Before Handover
item.description: As Egbetokun prepare hand over to Acting IGP Tunji Disu, the former IGP carry out last-minute redeployments wey affect several senior officers across Nigeria Police Force. According to internal police...

item.title: Thousands displaced after Quezon City shantytown fire
item.description: A devastating fire struck a densely populated shantytown along NIA Road in Barangay Pinyahan, Quezon City, on the night of March 6, 2026, leaving at least 4,000 families without homes. The fire starte...

item.title: European Nations Debate Responses to Surging Energy Costs Amid Global Tensions
item.description: European countries are confronting sharp disagreements over how to address skyrocketing energy prices triggered by ongoing conflicts in the Middle East and the lingering effects of the Ukraine war. Ga...

item.title: King Charles highlights global challenges in Commonwealth Day address
item.description: King Charles will deliver his annual Commonwealth Day message on Monday, addressing the 56 member nations of the Commonwealth during what he describes as 'a time of great challenge'. The monarch will ...

item.title: Cody Garbrandt wins UFC 326 despite multiple low blows
item.description: At UFC 326, former bantamweight champion Cody Garbrandt faced Chinese fighter Xiao Long in a tense contest. Garbrandt, seeking a comeback after two consecutive losses, began strongly in the first roun...

item.title: DOTr mandates cleaning and pest control for MRT-3 train after insect sightings
item.description: The Department of Transportation (DOTr) has directed the sanitation, disinfection, and pest control of the Metro Rail Transit 3 (MRT-3) train sets following reports and a viral photo showing small ins...

item.title: Six-Year-Old Schoolgirl Killed in Delta Road Accident
item.description: Na so wahala reach Delta State as one six-year-old girl, Oghenekome Edeburu, wey dem dey call Kome, die after one Julius Berger construction tipper truck run am down as she dey return from school with...

item.title: Emma Watson Seen Enjoying Romance with Mexican Tech Entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Bailleres
item.description: Emma Watson, the 35-year-old actress famed for her role as Hermione Granger, appears to have entered a romantic relationship with Mexican tech entrepreneur Gonzalo Hevia Bailleres. Watson, who has rec...

item.title: Two Candidates with Same Name Claim UPSC CSE 2025 Rank 301, Causing Identity Confusion
item.description: In the UPSC CSE 2025 results, a peculiar situation has emerged where two candidates, both named Akanksha Singh, have reportedly claimed the same rank of 301. This has triggered an identity verificatio...

item.title: Former Israeli Prime Minister Warns of Strategic Threat from Turkey
item.description: Former Israeli Prime Minister Naftali Bennett recently issued strong statements regarding Turkey and its president, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, labeling Ankara as a new strategic threat to Israel's security...

item.title: The Growing Vulnerability of Data Centers in Military Conflicts
item.description: Recent attacks in Iran have highlighted the strategic vulnerability of data centers in modern warfare. As critical infrastructure for both economic and governmental operations, these facilities are in...

item.title: Rising Costs and Alternatives for End-of-Life Services in Saskatchewan
item.description: Funeral costs in Saskatchewan have risen significantly, with traditional services often exceeding $20,000. Families are feeling the financial strain when planning end-of-life ceremonies, which can inc...

item.title: Brodie Kostecki takes Supercars lead after first-corner crash sidelines Broc Feeney
item.description: Brodie Kostecki has taken the lead in the Supercars championship following a dramatic opening-corner crash at Albert Park that ended Broc Feeney’s weekend early. The incident involved at least ten car...

item.title: Durban Film City project moves closer to reality after decades of delays
item.description: Plans for the Durban Film City, a proposed R7.5 billion world-class film studio complex, are gaining renewed attention after nearly 30 years of delays. The development is set on the former 21-hectare ...

item.title: Government Mobilises Emergency Teams Following Widespread Flooding in Kenya
item.description: Heavy rains across Kenya in early March 2026 caused widespread flooding, leading to the loss of at least 25 lives and the displacement of over 800 households. Nairobi was hardest hit, with 23 fataliti...

item.title: Dubai Faces Shortages in Fresh Food Supplies Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Dubai, a major hub in the Gulf region, is facing a temporary shortage of fresh food supplies due to disruptions in global logistics and heightened geopolitical tensions. Stefan Paul, director of Kuehn...
